channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Maruti And Toyota Say E20 Is Safe For Pre-2023 Cars. We Are Not Buying The Full Story
item.description: Maruti and Toyota say E20 petrol causes no damage to pre-2023 cars designed for E10. The Car Jury on what owners should actually watch for.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Vision S Interior Spied: BE6 Steering Signals Sub-4m With Big Car Kit
item.description: Mahindra Vision S sub-4m SUV interior spied with BE6-style steering and modern cabin, hinting at a serious Nexon and Brezza rival for 2027.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV 75 RWD Makes Less Power Than 63 kWh: The Odd Math Explained
item.description: Tata's Sierra EV 75 kWh RWD makes 209 PS vs the 63 kWh's 238 PS. Tata says it's an intentional packaging call, not a spec sheet error.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Windsor EV Chases 70% Localisation: Smart Move, But Buyers Should Still Wait
item.description: JSW MG Motor targets over 70% localisation on the Windsor EV after 75,000 sales in 21 months. Here is what it means for buyers eyeing the electric MPV.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: New Toyota Hilux Lands in India on 28 July: Tougher Face, Same Ladder Frame
item.description: Toyota confirms the 9th-gen Hilux for India on 28 July 2026 with a sharper front end, new tech and the same IMV ladder-frame underneath.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Slashes Up To Rs 3.35 Lakh Off Its EVs: Inventory Clearance Before Sierra EV Push
item.description: Tata Motors is offering up to Rs 3.35 lakh off on Harrier EV, Nexon EV, Curvv EV, Punch EV and Tiago EV this July 2026. Here is our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Teases Syros EV And Sorento Hybrid: The Electrified Push India Actually Needs
item.description: Kia India has teased both a BEV and an HEV, pointing to the Syros EV this month and a Sorento hybrid three-row SUV to follow.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes Workers Rally Against Cost Cuts: What It Means for Indian Buyers
item.description: Thousands of Mercedes workers protested cost-cutting plans in Germany, signalling wider trouble for the country's auto industry. Our take on the fallout.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Elevate Gets Rs 2.15 Lakh Off In July 2026: Grab It Or Hold?
item.description: Honda Elevate carries benefits up to Rs 2.15 lakh in July 2026, the highest across Honda's lineup. The Car Jury's take on whether to sign now.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: June 2026 Sales: Tata Punch And Nexon Lock Top Two, Maruti Still Owns The Ten
item.description: Tata Punch and Nexon topped June 2026 passenger vehicle sales in India, but Maruti placed five models in the Top 10. TCJ's read.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Harrier EV gets Rs 2.75 lakh off in July: grab it now
item.description: Tata is offering up to Rs 2.75 lakh off the Harrier EV and up to Rs 3.35 lakh off the Curvv EV in July 2026. Here is the full breakdown.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: World Car Awards Tops Global Visibility Rankings, But Does It Move Indian Metal?
item.description: World Car Awards named most visible auto awards programme for the 14th year running, reaching 49.3 crore people in 2025-26 across five continents.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XEV 9e Cineluxe Adds Satin Desert Myst: A Third Colour for the Flagship EV
item.description: Mahindra adds a new Satin Desert Myst finish to the XEV 9e Cineluxe Edition, joining Satin Black and Satin White in the flagship EV's lineup.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Kharkhoda plant goes live: 10 lakh cars a year is the real threat to rivals
item.description: Maruti Suzuki opens its 800-acre Kharkhoda plant in Haryana. Capacity will double to 10 lakh units. What it means for buyers and rivals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Curvv gets Rs 1.2 lakh off in July 2026: worth grabbing?
item.description: Tata Curvv gets up to Rs 1.2 lakh in cash, exchange and loyalty benefits in July 2026. The Car Jury's take on whether the coupe-SUV is a buy now.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai Creta Electric drops to Rs 10.99 lakh with BaaS: clever pricing, watch the maths
item.description: Hyundai Creta Electric now starts at Rs 10.99 lakh under Battery-as-a-Service, plus Rs 3.90 per km. Our verdict on whether the sticker cut is real.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: July 2026 Launches: Renault Kwid Facelift Leads A Thin Month
item.description: Six new cars are due in July 2026, from the Renault Kwid facelift to the BMW iX3. Here is what to buy, what to skip and what to wait for.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota July 2026 Price Hike: Up To Rs 33,000 On Hyryder, Glanza, Taisor, Rumion
item.description: Toyota raises prices on Hyryder, Glanza, Taisor and Rumion from July 2026, with hikes going up to Rs 33,000 depending on variant.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Sales June 2026: Domestic Up 13.5%, Exports Explode 600%
item.description: Honda Cars India posts 13.5% domestic growth in June 2026 and a 600% export surge, with the City facelift and ZR-V incoming.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Hikes Prices Up To Rs 39,000: Seltos Stings The Most From July 1
item.description: Kia India has raised prices on Sonet, Seltos and Carens Clavis from July 1, 2026, by up to Rs 38,500. Here is what buyers now pay.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: EV Sales Nearly Double In Q1 FY2026: Tata And Mahindra Own The Market
item.description: India's electric passenger vehicle registrations grew 89.3% in Q1 FY2026 to 82,737 units, with Tata and Mahindra taking over 63% share.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia June 2026: 24,552 Units, Best-Ever H1 At 1.63 Lakh, Seltos Still Doing The Heavy Lifting
item.description: Kia India posts 24,552 units in June 2026, up 19% YoY, and closes H1 at 1.63 lakh units. TCJ verdict on what is really driving the numbers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti WagonR BioFlex Sells Just 3 Units: Flex-Fuel's Infra Problem Exposed
item.description: Maruti WagonR BioFlex has recorded only three registrations since launch, exposing India's thin E85 pump network as the real bottleneck.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW MG June 2026 Sales Hit Record 7,568 Units, EVs Own 75% Of The Mix
item.description: JSW MG posted its highest-ever monthly wholesales in June 2026 at 7,568 units, up 29.83% YoY, with EVs contributing over 75% of volume.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Suzuki XL7 Facelift Leaks: A Preview Of The Maruti XL6's Next Face
item.description: Suzuki XL7 facelift images have leaked ahead of the 2026 GIIAS debut, previewing the styling India's Maruti XL6 is likely to inherit next.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Slavia Facelift Lands In August 2026: Overdue, But Worth The Wait
item.description: Skoda confirms the Slavia facelift will launch in August 2026 with a new grille, updated LED lighting and a mild price bump. Here is our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai June 2026 Sales Slip 10% After Supplier Fire: A One-Month Blip, Not A Trend
item.description: Hyundai India domestic sales fell 9.97% YoY in June 2026 after a supplier fire cost 13,900 units. The Car Jury reads the numbers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Seltos GTX(O), X-Line(O) launched at Rs 21.57 lakh with Level 2+ ADAS
item.description: Kia adds GTX(O) and X-Line(O) trims to the Seltos with 28 ADAS features, priced from Rs 21.57 lakh. The Car Jury's take on whether it's worth it.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault And Nissan Line Up 4 Hybrid SUVs: Maruti's Grip On Strong Hybrids Ends
item.description: Renault and Nissan are readying four strong-hybrid SUVs for India, led by the Duster Hybrid, with segment-best battery packs and 25 kmpl claims.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: KKR's $400M Bet on JSW MG: Chinese Baggage Finally Gets Priced Out
item.description: KKR is set to invest up to $400 million in JSW MG Motor India at a $3 billion valuation, diluting SAIC and making the JV majority Indian-owned.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata's Sierra EV And Harrier EV Bet Pays Off, But Only If Pricing Holds
item.description: Tata says Sierra EV and Harrier EV are pulling new buyers into the EV market as India enters the early majority phase. Our verdict on the strategy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes brings its 140-year S-Class world tour to India in July
item.description: Three Mercedes-Benz S-Class saloons will tour India from July as part of the brand's global 140-year, 60,000 km celebration drive.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes brings its 140-year S-Class world tour to India in July
item.description: Mercedes-Benz brings its 140 Years 140 Places transcontinental S-Class drive to India in July 2026. The Car Jury on what it actually signals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mumbai-Vadodara Expressway Opens August 31: Honda City Owners, Rejoice
item.description: The 157km Mumbai-Vadodara Expressway opens by August 31, 2026, cutting travel time to four hours. What it means for highway car buyers in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen's 100,000 Job Cuts and Plant Closures: India Impact Read
item.description: Volkswagen weighs 100,000 job cuts, four German plant closures and a passenger-car carve-out. What it means for Indian buyers of Taigun and Virtus.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Indian-owned 1927 Hispano-Suiza H6C wins Best of Show at Schlumpf Concours
item.description: A 1927 Hispano-Suiza H6C from the Pranlal Bhogilal Collection has won Best of Show at the inaugural Schlumpf Concours in Mulhouse, France.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Kwid Facelift Lands July 3: Dacia Spring Looks Hit India
item.description: Renault confirms the 2026 Kwid facelift for July 3 launch in India, with Dacia Spring-inspired styling taking on the Maruti S-Presso.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Discounts Dry Up As GST Cut Pulls Small-Car Buyers Back
item.description: Maruti and Tata are slashing discounts on Wagon R, Baleno, Tiago and Punch as the GST cut revives small-car demand. Here is what it means for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: July 2026 Launch Slate: Kwid Facelift Leads A Thin Month
item.description: Renault Kwid facelift headlines July 2026 launches, with Nissan, JSW-MG, Maruti, Skoda, Honda and Toyota also lining up new models. Here is the slate.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Brezza Facelift Lands 23 July Without ADAS: Maruti Reads The Room Wrong
item.description: Maruti Brezza facelift launches 23 July 2026 with a likely 1.0L turbo from Fronx, but no ADAS. The Car Jury verdict on whether to wait.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Vision S Spied With Production LED DRLs: The Real Face Emerges
item.description: Mahindra Vision S has been spied in Mussoorie with production-spec LED DRLs, confirming the circular headlights on earlier mules were placeholders.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Ninth-Gen Toyota Hilux Lands In India Next Month: Premium Inbound
item.description: Toyota will launch the ninth-gen Hilux in India next month with sharper design, 48V mild hybrid tech and a price hike over the current Rs 28.52 lakh base.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Brezza update lands July 23 with Fronx turbo: about time
item.description: Maruti will reveal the updated Brezza on July 23, 2026, with the Fronx's 1.0 turbo-petrol, fresh styling and new features. Our verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Bets On Five Startups For AI And Battery Recycling: Useful Or Just PR?
item.description: Maruti Suzuki onboards five startups under MSIP cohort five to co-develop battery recycling, AI and automation solutions with IIM-B's NSRCEL.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Kodiaq RS: Sold Out In Six Minutes, And Worth The Wait List
item.description: Skoda Kodiaq RS launches July 2 in India with all allocated units already booked. The Car Jury on whether the second batch is worth chasing.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: India's Connected Car Cybersecurity Rules Arrive: Overdue, Not Optional
item.description: MoRTH drafts mandatory cybersecurity and software-update rules for connected vehicles in India, with phased rollout from October 2026.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton Rear Leaks Ahead of July 9 Debut: Baby Patrol Look Confirmed
item.description: Nissan Tekton rear design appears undisguised in a leaked TVC shoot image, matching official teasers ahead of the July 9 debut.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Bets the Farm on EVs: 4 New Models, 10+ Refreshes by FY31
item.description: Tata Motors plans four new EVs including Sierra.ev and an Avinya-based model, plus 10+ refreshes by FY31, targeting 30% EV penetration.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VW Shareholder Wants China-Designed Cars Built In Germany: A Symptom, Not A Fix
item.description: A key VW shareholder wants the carmaker to build its China-developed models in Germany to save jobs. The Car Jury on why this misreads the problem.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Maruti Brezza Facelift: July 23 Launch, But Wait For The Turbo
item.description: Maruti will launch the 2026 Brezza facelift on July 23 with a new 1.0 turbo-petrol, ventilated seats and an underbody CNG tank. Our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
